content:
Though there is a lot to take away from this story, to me at least this isn't so much a story of determination and strength. Picking it apart I find the story itself to be actually a very sad one that perhaps not aged well with the change of time. At the time of me writing this this short story is reaching 84 years of age after all.

from Week 5 Discussion
Feb 9, 2025 4:37PM
Brandon Yen
"How does Eudora Welty use symbolism to convey themes of perseverance and sacrifice?"

Not well honestly. In my personal opinion this is actually a very sad story once it's picked apart and boiled down. All the details brought about only to bring in a beauty of the idea of the setting, it's nice to bring in the beauty of the mundanity of a simple old woman going along a path she's gone dozens of times. The main character, after balancing and walking her way over a log over a river she sits down to rest "She did not dare to close her eyes, and when a little boy brought her a plate with a slice of marble cake on it she poke to him. "that would be acceptable," she said. But when she went to take it there was just her own hand in the air." This is a much better bit of symbolism in the story because it tells me as a reader just how close this woman is to slipping away into "the last goodnight."
